brand_dna.category: Heritage-inspired apparel brand selling literary, dark-academic staples - crewnecks, oversized tees, and knits - in limited drops.
brand_dna.core_belief: Clothing should be lived in and carry your story forward: 'Every sweater, tee, and jacket is designed not just to be worn, but to be lived in - carrying your story forward.'
proof_points: Heavyweight 100% cotton fabrics with stated weights (275gsm tees, 330gsm varsity tee, 400gsm french terry crewneck)
Limited-run, made-to-order drops to reduce environmental impact
Beyond The First Day giving program: donations that provide books, school supplies, and student care items
Founded by twin sisters Jenny and Jill Kleinknecht, conceptualized 2023, named in New England 2024
brand_dna.positioning: Premium, small-batch and intentional - 'slow, intentional fashion' at an elevated price point, not fast, mass-market basics.

voice_pillars.name: Literary and Nostalgic
voice_pillars.desc: Writes like a story chapter, leaning on books, heritage, and quiet romanticism.
voice_pillars.quote: Briar Road was born from a love of literature, design, and the quiet art of intentional living.

voice_pillars.name: Lived-In Ease
voice_pillars.desc: Frames pieces as familiar, effortless staples meant for everyday wear.

voice_pillars.name: Academic Elegance
voice_pillars.desc: Blends prep and dark-academic references with a refined, considered tone.

voice_pillars.name: Intentional and Slow
voice_pillars.desc: Owns its no-returns, limited-drop model as a deliberate, values-led choice.
voice_pillars.quote: Thanks for understanding and supporting slow, intentional fashion.

phrases: relaxed, oversized fit for a lived-in feel
made for those who love quiet mornings by the lake with a book
a chapter; a small part of a life well-told

icp: Design-minded readers and creatives, largely women, who value heritage, storytelling, and quiet intentional living, and who buy elevated everyday staples they can wear for years.

personas.name: The Dark-Academic Reader
personas.description: A book-loving aesthete drawn to library evenings and literary references - moved by story-rich, nostalgic copy over discounts.

personas.name: The Slow-Fashion Believer
personas.description: A values-driven buyer who prefers limited, made-to-order pieces and is reassured by the low-impact, intentional-production angle.

personas.name: The Cozy Staple Collector
personas.description: Wants heavyweight, oversized everyday basics that feel lived-in - persuaded by fabric weight, fit, and comfort details.

offers: Free shipping on domestic orders over $200
Beyond The First Day donation add-on at checkout ($1.50 to $100 tiers)
Restock notifications via 'Notify Me When It's Available' on sold-out sizes
No returns or exchanges; all sale items final sale (limited-run model)

allowed.claim: Made from heavyweight 100% cotton fabric (e.g. 275gsm, 330gsm, 400gsm french terry).
Free shipping on domestic orders over $200.
Limited-run, made to reduce environmental impact.
Founded by twin sisters, conceptualized in 2023 and named in New England in 2024.
Relaxed, oversized unisex fit; runs true to size, size up for more oversized.
Donations through Beyond The First Day support students with books and supplies.

banned.claim: The best quality heritage apparel in the world / #1 dark-academic brand.
banned.why: FTC - unqualified superlative that cannot be substantiated.
FTC Green Guides - unqualified environmental claim; only the specific 'limited-run to reduce impact' language is supported.

banned.claim: Selling out fast - only a few left, order now (invented urgency).
banned.why: Fake urgency banned; stock scarcity must be real, not manufactured.

banned.claim: Loved by thousands / rated 5 stars by hundreds of customers.
banned.why: Invented review counts banned; no customer reviews exist in the inputs.

banned.claim: Guaranteed to last a lifetime / never fades or shrinks.
banned.why: Guaranteed-results claim without substantiation is prohibited.
Origin claim contradicts site copy ('Proudly made in China'); would be deceptive.
